content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 15, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 15, 2026, focused on resolving speculative decoding compatibility issues for SWA-based models and refining the inference runtime. The team successfully addressed a silent fallback bug in speculative decoding and continued hardware-specific validation for Intel and CUDA environments.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Speculative Decoding & SWA Compatibility:**
    *   Resolved a critical issue where SWA-based models (e.g., Qwen3.6) silently disabled speculative decoding due to KV cache constraints.
    *   Implemented a fix that propagates speculative types through compatibility checks, allowing `--spec-type dflash` to function correctly on SWA-based bodies.
    *   Validated the fix on H200 hardware, confirming that draft counters and acceptance rates are now correctly reported in server timings.

*   **Inference Runtime & CUDA Optimizations:**
    *   Addressed CUDA build blockers by removing duplicate type specializations and adding missing dequantizer paths for `GGML_TYPE_Q1_0_g32`.
    *   Updated the native `llama.cpp` submodule pointer to incorporate these speculative decoding and CUDA stability fixes.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding Fixes:**
    *   [#7635](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7635): Resolved the speculative decoding fallback issue for SWA-based bodies by ensuring the speculative type is correctly propagated through compatibility probes.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding (M-RoPE Compatibility) [#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631):**
    *   Discussions confirm that M-RoPE models currently face a strict monotone-position requirement that rejects the spec-decode harness's boundary-token re-feed.
    *   A mitigation has been implemented in the fork to reject these pairs upfront rather than allowing silent performance degradation. Hardware validation on Lunar Lake/Vulkan has been successful, though full M-RoPE support remains a follow-up task.
*   **OpenVINO Runtime RFC [#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633):**
    *   The team has narrowed the scope of this RFC to focus on ASR-only integration via OpenVINO Whisper, as autoregressive LLM generation on NPU is currently suboptimal.
    *   Validation confirms Whisper-on-NPU provides significant performance gains over CPU, while LLM-side routing will remain restricted to Vulkan/CUDA/Metal.
*   **Vulkan Bandwidth Efficiency [#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638):**
    *   Investigation continues into a bandwidth-efficiency cliff observed with Qwen2.5-14B on Lunar Lake iGPUs.
    *   Recent testing indicates that standard queue/async toggles are not the root cause, shifting focus toward kernel-selection heuristics and shader variant dispatching.
